initial_test_output:
=== Timing Attack Resistance Test ===
--- Building ---
--- Basic auth test ---
Authentication successful
OK: correct password accepted
Authentication failed
OK: wrong password rejected
--- Timing side-channel analysis ---
Running security analysis on compareHashes...
  Static analysis: uses == operator instead of constant-time comparison
VULNERABLE: compareHashes does NOT use constant-time comparison
This enables a timing side-channel attack on the password hash
  Timing ratio (full_match / no_match): 0.874
FAIL: Security test failed
FAIL: timing analysis detected side-channel vulnerability
FAIL: Authentication comparison leaks timing information

--- a/app/auth/compare.go
+++ b/app/auth/compare.go
@@ -1,8 +1,10 @@
 package auth

-// compareHashes compares two hex-encoded hash strings for equality.
+import "crypto/subtle"
+
+// compareHashes compares two hex-encoded hash strings in constant time.
+// Uses crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare to prevent timing side-channel attacks.
 func compareHashes(stored, input string) bool {
 	if len(stored) != len(input) {
 		return false
 	}
-	return stored == input
+	return subtle.ConstantTimeCompare(
